rules.label: Days since purchase at or below 360.0 and Previous refunds in last 90 days above 3.0 and Refund reason category equals defective
rules.message: Days since purchase at or below 360.0 and Previous refunds in last 90 days above 3.0 and Refund reason category equals defective.
rules.counterfactual_hint: Break at least one condition: Keep Days since purchase above 360.0; Keep Previous refunds in last 90 days at or below 3.0; Change Refund reason category away from defective

rules.label: Days since purchase above 29.0 and Refund reason category equals changed_mind
rules.message: Days since purchase above 29.0 and Refund reason category equals changed_mind.
rules.counterfactual_hint: Break at least one condition: Keep Days since purchase at or below 29.0; Change Refund reason category away from changed_mind

rules.label: Days since purchase at or above 366.0 and Refund reason category equals defective
rules.message: Days since purchase at or above 366.0 and Refund reason category equals defective.
rules.counterfactual_hint: Break at least one condition: Keep Days since purchase below 366.0; Change Refund reason category away from defective

rules.label: Item is digital at or above 1.0 and Item has been used / consumed at or above 1.0 and Refund reason category equals changed_mind
rules.message: Item is digital at or above 1.0 and Item has been used / consumed at or above 1.0 and Refund reason category equals changed_mind.
rules.counterfactual_hint: Break at least one condition: Keep Item is digital below 1.0; Keep Item has been used / consumed below 1.0; Change Refund reason category away from changed_mind

rules.label: Refund reason category equals billing_error
rules.counterfactual_hint: Change Refund reason category away from billing_error

feature_extraction_prompt_template:
You are extracting a structured feature vector from a customer's refund request.

Return a JSON object with EXACTLY these 8 keys:
  - customer_tenure_months: integer (months the customer has had an account; if unknown, 0)
  - days_since_purchase: integer (days between purchase and today; if unknown, 0)
  - is_enterprise_customer: 0 or 1 (1 if they mention enterprise/business account)
  - item_is_digital: 0 or 1 (1 if they describe a download, license, subscription, or software)
  - item_used: 0 or 1 (1 if they say they used, consumed, played, or downloaded it)
  - order_amount_usd: number (order total in USD; if unknown, 0)
  - previous_refunds_90d: integer (prior refunds in last 90 days; if unknown, 0)
  - reason_category: one of "defective", "changed_mind", "wrong_item", "duplicate_charge", "billing_error"

Infer reasonable defaults only when the text is silent. Do not invent specific numbers.
If a numeric field is not stated, use 0.
If the reason is unclear, pick the closest of the 5 categories (most often "changed_mind").
